input.command:
echo "=== A. ANY wine process system-wide (not just LaunchCat) ==="
ps aux | grep -iE 'wine|wineserver|wine64|wineboot|winedevice|explorer\.exe|services\.exe|rpcss|plugplay|svchost' | grep -v grep | grep -vi 'grep' || echo "  ✅ none anywhere"

echo
echo "=== B. wineserver socket/temp dirs (left behind after kill) ==="
ls -lad /tmp/.wine-* /private/tmp/.wine-* /var/folders/*/*/*/.wine-* 2>/dev/null || echo "  (none found in common temp locations)"
# wine keeps a per-user server dir; the socket lives under it
find /tmp /private/tmp -maxdepth 1 -name '.wine-*' 2>/dev/null | while read d; do echo "  socket dir: $d"; ls -la "$d" 2>/dev/null; done

echo
echo "=== C. confirm the 99% CPU is gone (load avg) ==="
uptime | sed 's/.*load/load/'
echo "top CPU consumers now:"
ps -axo pcpu,pid,comm | sort -rn | head -4

echo
echo "=== D. lingering CoH/wine drive mounts? (from earlier dosdevices auto-map) ==="
ls /Volumes/ 2>/dev/null | grep -iE 'CoHMod|App Cleaner' && echo "  ^ still mounted (harmless; eject if unused)" || echo "  ✅ no CoH-related volumes mounted"
input.description: Sweep for system-wide wine procs, sockets, CPU, mounts
